description: Address semantic review findings: outbox FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, cache DEV_AUTH_ENABLED at startup, wire maintenance tasks into worker, consistent 404 detection in object storage, add metrics calls to worker runtime.
steps: Fix outbox double-dispatch: change poll_unprocessed() to use FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED pattern similar to job_queue.rs
Cache DEV_AUTH_ENABLED at startup using std::sync::LazyLock
Wire timeout_stale_jobs() and cleanup_expired() into the worker main loop
Make object storage 404 detection consistent with a shared helper
Add metrics calls to worker_runtime.rs for job processing
acceptance_criteria: poll_unprocessed uses FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to prevent double-dispatch
DEV_AUTH_ENABLED is read from env once and cached
Worker periodically runs timeout_stale_jobs and session cleanup
Object storage uses a single is_not_found_error helper in both download and exists
record_job_processed is called after each job completes in worker_runtime
findings: All 49 tests pass (17 api, 12 app, 20 platform). Build, clippy (-D warnings), fmt all pass with SQLX_OFFLINE=true. Used std::sync::OnceLock instead of LazyLock for DEV_AUTH_ENABLED cache because the project MSRV is 1.75 and LazyLock was only stabilized in 1.80 (clippy::incompatible_msrv caught this). OnceLock::get_or_init provides equivalent lazy initialization behavior. The outbox FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED required restructuring the query to use a subquery pattern matching job_queue.rs.
